# mehmetfahriozmen.dev

## About
Personal website of Mehmet Fahri Özmen — Backend Systems Architect & Engineering Leader based in İzmir, Turkey. 12+ years building scalable distributed systems across e-commerce, ad-tech, edtech, and productivity.

## Author
- Name: Mehmet Fahri Özmen
- Role: Backend Systems Architect & Engineering Leader
- Location: İzmir, Turkey
- Education: Computer Engineering, Yaşar University (2006-2011)
- LinkedIn: https://linkedin.com/in/mfozmen
- GitHub: https://github.com/mfozmen
- X/Twitter: https://x.com/mfozmen
- Email: contact@mehmetfahriozmen.dev

## Key Expertise
- Backend architecture (Node.js, .NET, Golang, Java)
- Distributed systems (Kafka, RabbitMQ, microservices)
- Cloud infrastructure (AWS, Azure, Docker, Kubernetes)
- Databases (PostgreSQL, Elasticsearch, Redis, MSSQL)
- Engineering leadership (team building, hiring, mentoring)
- E-commerce, ad-tech, edtech, productivity domains

## Pages
- Homepage: https://mehmetfahriozmen.dev — Overview, galaxy visualization of systems, featured projects
- About: https://mehmetfahriozmen.dev/about — Career story, philosophy, personal interests
- CV: https://mehmetfahriozmen.dev/cv — Full professional timeline, skills, education
- Writing: https://mehmetfahriozmen.dev/writing — Blog on engineering leadership
- Contact: https://mehmetfahriozmen.dev/contact — Contact form and direct channels

## Notable Projects
- Mobilet: Event ticketing platform, 80K tickets/hour peak capacity
- VillaSepeti: Vacation rental marketplace with set-theory-based search engine
- Shubuo: Digital advertising platform with contract-aware ad scheduling
- MagicPags: Educational platform with real-time learning analytics
- BeforeSunset AI: Productivity assistant, backend built from scratch
- Decktopus AI: Presentation platform with serverless architecture

## Writing
- "The Hardest Refactor: From Engineer to Manager" — On the transition from engineer to engineering leader, covering perfectionism traps, team building with personality diversity (explorer/finisher framework), and the distinction between management and leadership.
